findings.rationale: The upstream class describes general packaging details, whereas our class specifies a particular tier within the consumption hierarchy.
findings.qaRationale: PackagingTier is a classification of a packaging's position in the consumption hierarchy (sales/grouped/transport), not a bundle of descriptive packaging attributes like gs1:PackagingDetails, so it is a different concept rather than a narrower one.

findings.rationale: PackagingTier is a specific position within the packaging hierarchy, whereas the upstream Packaging class represents any packaging item.
findings.qaRationale: PackagingTier is a classification of hierarchy position (sales/grouped/transport), not a packaging artifact, so it is a different kind of concept than the upstream Packaging entity class rather than a narrower subclass of it.

findings.rationale: Both classes represent a packaging item, but the upstream term is a generic packaging concept while ours is specific to PPWR regulation.
findings.qaRationale: Both are packaging classes, but ours is the PPWR-regulation-scoped subset of the upstream generic packaging concept, making ours narrower.

findings.rationale: Both classes describe packaging items and include details such as type, materials, and recycling information, but the OUR term is specifically tied to PPWR regulatory context while the GS1 class is a general packaging description.
findings.qaRationale: GS1 PackagingDetails is a generic packaging-description class while euppwr:Packaging is the same core concept narrowed to a PPWR-regulated packaging item carrying regulatory data points, so ours is the narrower regulatory specialization.
